compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The biome is experiencing a catastrophic collapse of vegetative matter. The P3 Pothos (anchor) is no longer visible in the current frame. The P1/P2/P4 specimens are either absent or reduced to non-viable soil debris.

plant_audit.P1: Pot is present but contains no visible plant matter; appears as bare, dark substrate.
plant_audit.P2: Pot is present but contains no visible plant matter; appears as bare, dark substrate.
plant_audit.P3: The white rabbit anchor is missing from the frame; the pot is empty/bare soil.
plant_audit.P4: No distinct specimen identified; soil surface appears uniform and devoid of life.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially over-saturated or degraded.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of crushed eggshell fragments on the surface of the black pot; these are identified as an 'Intervention' (likely calcium supplementation) that failed to support plant survival.
biome_observations.desk_surface: The white paper/surface area shows a small, dark, multi-legged object (likely a centipede or similar arthropod) in early frames, which has since vacated or been removed.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single leaf in the black pot and a white rabbit anchor in the P3 pot.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: Stagnation; no growth observed. The leaf in the black pot shows signs of chlorosis/wilting.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Total loss of visibility. The lighting has shifted to a low-intensity, high-contrast state, rendering the pots as dark voids. The biological specimens have effectively vanished from the visual record.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has transitioned from a state of 'Stressed/Declining' to 'Systemic Failure'. The lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue in the final frames confirms the death or removal of all registered specimens.
anomalies: Total disappearance of the white rabbit scale anchor
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a rapid decline of the indoor botanical collection. Over the 5-day observation period, the specimens exhibited progressive wilting followed by total disappearance. The intervention of eggshells suggests an attempt to remediate soil health, but this proved insufficient. The current state is one of complete biological vacancy.
